title: Getting 403 Forbidden on GCP
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

For the past few weeks I started getting this error on GCP:

`ERROR: [youtube] FspXoIiuiFo: Sign in to confirm you’re not a bot. Use --cookies-from-browser or --cookies for the authentication. See  https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#how-do-i-pass-cookies-to-yt-dlp  for how to manually pass cookies. Also see  https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/Extractors#exporting-youtube-cookies  for tips on effectively exporting YouTube cookies`

I tried to pass the cookies but it didn't change anything. 

When I set up VPC with reserved IP, it changes to:

`ERROR: unable to download video data: HTTP Error 403: Forbidden`

I use this to get the latest version:

```
# Upgrade pip and install yt-dlp from GitHub
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade git+https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp.git
```

I tried different GCP regions and different egress IPs and nothing worked. 
This being said, everything works just fine on my local. 

Any idea what's going on? 


### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell

```
author: aminrabinia

title: [Announcement] Deprecation of various options
body:
yt-dlp has accumulated several useless, broken, or partially deprecated options over the years.
We want to do some house cleaning and finally remove some of these.

The first step will be a deprecation period of at least 6 months. During this time, the options will continue to exist in yt-dlp, but their functionality will be removed and replaced by a deprecation warning. This should give users sufficient advance notice to update their configs/scripts.

After the 6-month deprecation period has passed, we may fully remove any or all of these options. This means trying to use the options will result in an error.

Users who have these options in their configs/scripts/applications will need to remove them.


Below is a list of options that will be deprecated, and why:

______

- `--xattr-set-filesize`

The rationale for adding this one was questionable to begin with (see https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/1176).
This is a very hacky workaround for a very niche use-case. It's so niche that nobody noticed this option has been broken on most downloads for years.

______

- `--dump-user-agent`

This option is either out-of-scope (yt-dlp is not a random user-agent generator) or misleading/harmful: if the UA that yt-dlp used is needed for an external script/downloader, the correct way to handle this is to take **all** of the headers from the info dict's `http_headers` field instead.

______

- `--youtube-skip-dash-manifest`
- `--no-youtube-include-dash-manifest`
- `--youtube-skip-hls-manifest`
- `--no-youtube-include-hls-manifest`
- `--youtube-include-dash-manifest`
- `--no-youtube-skip-dash-manifest`
- `--youtube-include-hls-manifest`
- `--no-youtube-skip-hls-manifest`

These are site-specific and shouldn't be general options.
They have been labelled ["not recommended"](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#not-recommended) for years, and they've been replaced by [extractor arguments](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#extractor-arguments).

______

- `--youtube-print-sig-code`

Same as above. This is an option intended for developers, but is barely (if ever) used during modern debugging. Removing this will ease our maintenance burden, since it carries with it a large, complex chunk of code that isn't used for anything else.

______

- `--dump-headers`
- `--dump-intermediate-pages`

More developer options, but these are old unused aliases.

______

- `--sponskrub`
- `--no-sponskrub`
- `--sponskrub-cut`
- `--no-sponskrub-cut`
- `--sponskrub-force`
- `--no-sponskrub-force`
- `--sponskrub-location`
- `--sponskrub-args`

SponSkrub was deprecated in favor of [Sponsorblock](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#sponsorblock-options). These options were partially already raising a deprecation warning.

______

- `--avconv-location`
- `--prefer-avconv`
- `--no-prefer-ffmpeg`
- `--no-prefer-avconv`
- `--prefer-ffmpeg`

avconv support has already been removed. This would remove remaining code, and mark all options as deprecated.

______

- `-C`/`--call-home`
- `--no-call-home`
- `--include-ads`
- `--no-include-ads`
- `--write-annotations`
- `--no-write-annotations`

These options did nothing. We will not add telemetry. Ad downloading is out of scope for yt-dlp. Annotations no longer exist.

______

- `--cn-verification-proxy`

This option was an alias that was already raising a deprecation warning.
author: seproDev

title: Improve CONTRIBUTING.md Adding support for a new site chapter
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

In `CONTRIBUTING.md` there is a chapter called `Adding support for a new site`

It is good starting point but it could be improved. Namely the example dictionary that it returns is not yet enough to download video since it misses formats. Optionally you could also add thumbnail and tags. 

You could start this chapter with sample html page that contains tags in meta tag like `<meta property="video:tag" content="tag1, tag2, tag3">`

And sample video with thumbnail and multiple sources.
```html
<video controls width="640" poster="thumbnail.jpg">
  <source src="video-1080p.mp4" type="video/mp4" label="1080p">
  <source src="video-720p.mp4" type="video/mp4" label="720p">
  <source src="video-480p.mp4" type="video/mp4" label="480p">
  Your browser does not support the video tag.
</video>
```

And then say that: Assuming you have following html, this is how you can write extractor for it.

This way user would have more derailed example because writing formats on my own wasn't easy task. I had to read several extractors before I was able to understand what they want from me. 

You don't have to host actual video in this github repository, just have some dummy html so that people can run it in debugger and see the sample dictionary that contains all fields.

title: Wistia support suddenly broken
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

United States

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Wistia channels now fail to download properly when 1-2 months ago they were working correctly. No error is given, but 0 videos are detected.

Perhaps Wistia changed its channel layout? I see that now the javascript on wistia channels seems obfuscated in a simple fashion.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: nullchinchilla

title: [Instagram] Unsupported URL: instagram.com/reels/audio/<id>
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a site-specific feature
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

USA

### Example URLs

- https://www.instagram.com/reels/audio/725553827147040/
- https://www.instagram.com/reels/audio/3019916334835140/
- https://www.instagram.com/reels/audio/1500099298035233/
- https://www.instagram.com/reels/audio/1434226370954749/
- https://www.instagram.com/reels/audio/681005011577173/
- https://www.instagram.com/reels/audio/2158378074355222/

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

It appears that this kind of URL is not yet supported by yt-dlp. That's the TL;DR. Pretty simple; support for these pages (probably) just isn't implemented yet.

For context: each of the examples above is a song* that is used in / attached to one or more posts**. The song has its own dedicated page and URL, and that's accessible both through the mobile app and through a web browser.

- *: or otherwise the "original" audio snippet, if applicable. As in like, if people have Remixed the post or whatever, or if people made their own posts using that post's audio (using it as a "Template"?), or whatever.

- **: This goes for all types of posts that I checked; regular posts, _Reels_ posts, and _Story_ posts. I won't waste time going into detail about why I think this is noteworthy, so if you don't really know what I'm getting at just don't worry about it. It's not really important, just a subtle distinction.


Tangential note: apparently, for songs attached to regular posts, they aren't visible or audible when viewing that post on the web version of Instagram, but they _are_ visible and audible on the Instagram mobile app. This note does not apply to _Reels_ posts, which seem to work exactly the same as they do on the Instagram mobile app.

Additionally, and perhaps ironically, if you click on one of the _Reels_ posts that uses a given song / audio clip, and try passing that post's URL into yt-dlp, yt-dlp supports that no-problem! 
But that may be just because yt-dlp is retrieving the m4a audio file(s) specific to that one _Reels_ video post, even if there are 100 identical m4a audio files, as those are paired to their respective _Reels_ video posts... Though that's just speculation on my part. 
And that's not a proper workaround anyway, because a lot of the time that's not the full song / audio clip; I've almost never seen a post include the full song like that.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

This isn't video specific at all and has been present for at least 2 years minimum, and I'm pretty sure longer.  I'm assuming it's related to the fact that yt-dlp first downloads a webm for this audio and then changes the container to .ogg in some way, but I haven't tried to look into it past that.

title: [YouTube] Can't download some premium exclusive formats
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Prior to [yt-dlp 2025.07.21](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/releases/tag/2025.07.21) yt-dlp was able to download and merge premium account exclusive formats given a premium account cookies. However, I noticed that since 2025.08.11 these formats are no long available even with cookies.
I can't remember all the formats but at least high bitrate audio formats, namely [774 and 141](https://gist.github.com/MartinEesmaa/2f4b261cb90a47e9c41ba115a011a4aa#dash-audio) are missing. I can get format 356 the paywalled video format at the moment though.

I tried https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V9vuCByb6js and some other music videos. Formats I got are exactly the same as that without cookies

I'm stilling getting 774 on my iOS devices so it seems ios player client was removed from defaults? But the problem is ios client no long accept cookies file. 
Is there any way to download those premium formats without cookies without a PO token at the moment?


### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

title: [YouTube] Downloading sections of a video leaves blank space at the start
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I'm trying to download sections of a video and every time I do the output results in a lot of blank space at the beginning with audio still playing from those parts until it reaches the timestamps I specified. I tried to change the timestamps, but it gave the same result. Downloading a different video with the same and or different timestamps didn't do anything. Also, I tried to download the latest yt-dlp and its ffmpeg dependencies, but it didn't fix this problem. Downloading sections of videos like this was working before so I'm not sure what happened.

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)

title: Failing on 10Play
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)

title: Local timestamp to match server broke between 2024-03-23 and 2024-05-20
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

yt-dlp used to timestamp the downloaded file to match the server timestamp. This broke at some point between 2024-03-23 and 2024-05-20. On both those dates I had updated my exe to latest, and kept copies of them. Running them both right now on the same url, the older exe still performs the expected timestamping, while the newer one (and all since) leave the timestamp of the downloaded file at the current wall clock time.

I can try to isolate the change by looking through the git diff of aa7e9ae through 4ccd73f, but 125 files changed in that range and I've never looked at this code before. I'm reporting the issue before I finish investigation in case someone recognizes the problem more quickly, or can point me to a "won't fix" issue that I may have overlooked in my search for duplicates. I'll follow up if I learn anything more specific.

I'm runing Windows 10 Home 22H2 19045.6216 if that's relevant.

title: [Coub] A wrong video is downloaded
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Here's a deleted coub: https://coub.com/view/1313jl

I'm expecting yt-dlp to fail:

```
$ wget --no-verbose https://coub.com/view/1313jl
https://coub.com/view/1313jl:
2025-09-03 01:17:09 ERROR 404: Not Found.
```

But surprisingly, it's trying to download something:

```
$ yt-dlp -F https://coub.com/view/1313jl
[Coub] Extracting URL: https://coub.com/view/1313jl
[Coub] 1313jl: Downloading JSON metadata
[info] Available formats for 1313jl:
ID               EXT RESOLUTION │   FILESIZE PROTO │ VCODEC     ACODEC
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
html5-audio-med  mp3 audio only │  795.10KiB https │ audio only mp3
html5-audio-high mp3 audio only │    1.16MiB https │ audio only mp3
html5-video-med  mp4 unknown    │  716.32KiB https │ unknown    video only
html5-video-high mp4 unknown    │  716.32KiB https │ unknown    video only
```

This is caused by the Coub API which parses `1313`, ignores `jl` and returns info about a wrong video:

```
$ wget -q -O - https://coub.com/api/v2/coubs/1313jl.json | jq
{
  ...
  "id": 1313,
  "type": "Coub::Simple",
  "permalink": "a50swc",
  "title": "New Year at Gorky Park",
  ...
```

This is definitely a Coub API bug, but I think it would be nice if yt-dlp compared the expected coub id with the `permalink` value and refused to download the wrong video.

(the version mentioned below ↓ is wrong, the actual version I'm testing is https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/commit/d925e92b710153d0d51d030f115b3c87226bc0f0)

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: Tian0982

title: --sleep not working on thumbnail only download from playlist using --skip-download
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I want to download thumbnails only from a long playlist (more than 1000 entries).

I get blocked sometime between 300 and 400 thumbnail downloads using a proxy and `--extractor-args "youtube:player-client=default,tv_simply"`. So, I want to try to use `--sleep-interval 4 --max-sleep-interval 6` and see if that has any effect.

My command line:
```
yt-dlp-nb.exe --sleep-interval 4 --max-sleep-interval 6 --proxy "https://redacted:89" --extractor-args "youtube:player-client=default,tv_simply" --autonumber-size 4 -o "%(autonumber)s-%(title)s.%(ext)s" --skip-download --write-thumbnail --convert-thumbnails png "https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL63ZO-jXFTatssIiQjDHSa6X-WPu-EyYV"
```

Expected result: yt-dlp should sleep between 4 to 6 seconds between each entry in the playlist.

Actual result: yt-dlp ignores the `--sleep` switch and does not sleep at all.

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

Global

body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

I'm working on an API that downloads video audios on demand, to then get the transcription of the dialogues. The problem I'm having is that the Vimeo videos don't work when the API is deployed.

I'm passing username and password to ytdlp to login to Vimeo, and it works when running the API in my local environment. But when deployed, it fails to login. Apparently, Vimeo is expecting me to validate the login using a One Time Passcode because the request comes from a different IP, even tho I haven't activated Two Factor Authentication.

Since this is an API, I can't provide a cookie, as it will expire and would need to be renewed constantly. Is there any workaround? I tried not providing any credentials, but apparently they are needed to download the video. I've seen some people saying it is possible to download from Vimeo in an older version, would that work in this case?

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output


title: Single/minimal stage postprocessor
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I'm wanting to change this tool so that the postprocessor doesn't require calling ffmpeg and re-writing the entire file to disk for each thing that needs to be embedded in the final video file.

I'm looking through the `postprocesser` folder and I barely can make heads or tails of it. Apparently _just_ embedding a thumbnail requires 250 lines on top of the base class. And to make things worse there's basically zero documentation and Python doesn't have type hints.

At this point, I'm mainly asking for some guidance. Should I just rewrite this whole thing? Or is there someone who can give me a rundown on how it works.

What I basically want to do is have each extended PostProcessor class return a set of requirements for ffmpeg, including valid codecs, and channel mappings. Then something else would see how many of those can be combined. However as I understand the PostProcessor class just contains some helper functions, and each extended class calls ffmpeg, so adapting it would require changing the code at which each PostProcessor extended class is created, and its methods called.

### Region

United States

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

!! following playlist problem only happens with significant variables, it DOES work if I simply put: 
yt-dlp PLAYLIST-URL

There are 3 youtube **playlist** URL's that can be copied. 
1. thumbnail of playlist & text title of the playlist
2. below thumbnail, a link that says "view full playlist"

If you get the URL from the thumbnail or title(which is VERY intuitive to do, and for infrequent users, picking the right one is not memorable) it seems to have issues with the & in the URL.  

Here are my examples (they have been recreated with others.
my input prompt:

yt-dlp -f "bestvideo[height<=1080][ext=mp4]Bestaudio[ext=m4a]/best[height<=1080][ext=mp4]" --embed-thumbnail --embed-metadata --sponsorblock-remove all --write-description --embed-chapters --convert-thumbnails jpg --output-na-placeholder "" --yes-playlist --ignore-errors --download-archive "%USERPROFILE%\Downloads\YTDL\downloaded.txt" -o "%USERPROFILE%\Downloads\YTDL\%(playlist_title|Unknown Playlist)s - %(playlist_count|?)d\%(playlist_autonumber)02d_%(n_entries)d - %(title)s.%(ext)s" 

This works for what i want (new folder with playlist title - (total number of vids) thumbnails embeded, description in txt.

working - "view full playlist"
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLR8TbGWIhJJIiukJJlIZDlG7oG6zs5y4Y

not working - thumbnail & title link
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ep0PhwsFx0A&list=PLR8TbGWIhJJIiukJJlIZDlG7oG6zs5y4Y

NOW, the not-working one DOES work like this
"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ep0PhwsFx0A&list=PLR8TbGWIhJJIiukJJlIZDlG7oG6zs5y4Y"

I can't be bothered to remember to put "" around url, nor does it automate.  please fix the & problem in playlist DL.  
Thanks

author: rishiagrahari95

title: [YouTube] Can't download premium formats from playlists
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I'm trying to download a playlist from YouTube Music using my YT Premium account and using the premium formats 774 or 141. It applies to any playlist but here is one example:

https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_khFmuhQkzFx2o8qyXpIskoJW2aDwTqdIs
The first item of that playlist is https://music.youtube.com/watch?v=PeueTvU_hPA

I can download each item of the playlist individually without problem:

```sh
./yt-dlp_macos -vU --cookies cookies.txt -f 774 "https://music.youtube.com/watch?v=PeueTvU_hPA"
```

But when I try to download the whole playlist yt-dlp fails because it can't find the premium format (see verbose output):

```sh
./yt-dlp_macos --cookies cookies.txt -f 774 "https://youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_khFmuhQkzFx2o8qyXpIskoJW2aDwTqdIs"
```

So my best guess is that cookies are not used correctly when downloading playlist items.

It tried to find and fix the bug myself but wasn't successful :(

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

title: Can't download live subs from m3u8 stream
body:

title: [nebula] Login via username/password or .netrc is broken
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))

- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Germany

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I'm using yt-dlp with `--netrc` and the login entry for `machine watchnebula username <myusername> password <mypassword>` seemed to work in the past; however it currently no longer does. Access via `--cookies-from-browser` is still working though.

I did a bit of snopping around in the developer tools when logging in to nebula.tv with a browser, and the call to nebula.tv/auth/login now includes three JSON fields: `email`, `password` and `captcha_result`, the latter seems to come from hcaptcha. Not sure if we can do anything about that in NebulaBaseIE._perform_login()…

Another thing is that when `--netrc` along with `--cookies-from-browser` is given, the login info from netrc is preferred, however when it no longer works, yt-dlp just fails without trying the cookies from the browser session next; so at first I was confused and thought that the login in the browser was somehow broken. If netrc support is really broken and should be removed, maybe print a respective warning in _perform_login() so that users that still rely on netrc for other sites can remove the watchnebula entry from netrc.

Using yt-dlp commit 603acdff07f0 (2025-09-05 21:28:52, "[ie/charlierose] Fix extractor (#14231)").

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

title: [YouTube] Want to add "Premium" to filename based on "format_note"
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

`format_note` has the sub-string "Premium" when a video has such formats. However there's also other stuff there.

It seems like there's no conditional clauses or regex in `--output`, but there seems to be a workaround using `--parse-metadata` (if I'm wrong and it's possible to use `--output`, please let me know).

However, I can't seem to get it to work (Windows, batch file). This is my test batch file:
```
"yt-dlp.exe" --cookies "www.youtube.com_cookies.txt" --paths "D" --parse-metadata "format_note:%%(format_note_edit)s" --replace-in-metadata format_note_edit ".*( Premium).*" "\1" --output "%%(uploader)s/%%(upload_date>%%Y.%%m.%%d)s %%(title).148s %%(id)s %%(vcodec)s%%(format_note)s%%(format_note_edit)s.%%(ext)s" "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Plk3Vu6zAk8"
```
I get this message:
```
[debug] Searching for '(?P<format_note_edit>.+)' in '%(format_note)s'
[MetadataParser] Parsed format_note_edit from '%(format_note)s': 'NA'.
```
My pathetic attempts to use `--parse-metadata` are [based on this post](https://www.reddit.com/r/youtubedl/comments/mz0yxs/comment/gvy8361/). It states that `--parse-metadata "title:%(title1)s"` is supposed to copy `title` to `title1`.

What am I doing wrong?

title: [Twitch] Takes long time to remux/add metadata to videos from some streamers
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Sweden (but should work worldwide)

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Since about a week, downloads from some Twitch channels, such as huds601, will stall for a while on remuxing (if remuxing is used) and on metadata addition (if that is used). Normally when downloading from that channel, ffmpeg will fix the stream after downloading it but that doesn't happen anymore either. Normally, after the download is complete the process of fixing/remuxing/adding metadata takes a few seconds at most, now it takes much longer. On all others channels I've tested, the process is as usual. I have the newest versions of both yt-dlp and FFmpeg.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

author: hseg

title: New yt-dlp_linux releases have non-portable GLIBC_2.14 requirements the old ones didn't.
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Hi. Today I updated to the most recent release build of yt-dlp_linux. Unfortunately unlike prior very portable releases, this one now has a hard dependency,

(superkuh)-(jobs:0)-(~/video/youtube)
(! 2132)-> sudo yt-dlp_linux --update-to master -vU
yt-dlp_linux: /lib/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.14' not found (required by yt-dlp_linux)

It would be really cool if the feature or whatever added that changed this requirement, or the change in the build process for the linux static builds, would be aligned with the old way so yt-dlp_linux can run on retromachines (ie, distro from 2010~) that need yt-dlp the most because they have no other way of access youtube.

The version of yt-dlp_linux release from 2025-08-07, last month, worked just fine.

(! 2135)-> ldd --version
ldd (Ubuntu EGLIBC 2.11.1-0ubuntu7.21) 2.11.1

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell
yt-dlp_linux: /lib/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.14' not found (required by yt-dlp_linux)
```

author: Zomis

title: Only use cookies if the video cannot be downloaded without cookies
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Is there a way to only use cookies during bulk downloads (such as downloading a whole channel) if cookies are necessary to download a video (private, membership, needs login etc)

If I use cookies for every video I have a higher chance of getting my account rate limited. The videos that require cookies can be a small percentage of a channel.

Right now I have do multiple passes, I first run my download without a cookie to get all the videos I can that don't need a cookie, and then I run the same command with a cookie to get the rest.

Of course that's a lot of wasted time and calls. I'd rather have yt-dlp be more smart about when to use or not use a cookie.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output


author: WatashiSenseiGalirus

title: [platzi] Broken extractor: site migrated from /clases to /cursos; page no longer exposes data = {...}; JSON
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Colombia

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Platzi appears to have migrated (at least the Spanish site) from URLs under **/clases** to **/cursos**. The current extractor’s **_VALID_URL** expects:
# current in extractor/platzi.py (before local test changes)
```
_VALID_URL = r'''(?x)
    https?://
        (?:
            platzi\.com/clases|           # es version
            courses\.platzi\.com/classes  # en version
        )/(?P<id>[^/?\#&]+)
'''
```
On today’s site, Spanish course lesson URLs follow:
`https://platzi.com/cursos/<courseSlug>/<materialSlug>/
`
As a quick local test, I modified _VALID_URL so the extractor recognizes /cursos and the new slug structure:

```
# local test only
_VALID_URL = r'''(?x)
    https?://
        (?:
            platzi\.com/cursos|           # es version (new)
            courses\.platzi\.com/classes  # en version (legacy)
        )/[^/]+/(?P<id>\d+)-[^/?\#&]+
'''
```
With that change, URL matching succeeds, but extraction then fails because the page no longer contains the previously expected inline JSON (data = {...};). The extractor calls:

`self._search_regex(r'data\s*=\s*({.+?})\s*;', webpage, 'data')`

which raises `RegexNotFoundError`. Response headers suggest a modern Next.js / RSC stack (e.g.,` Vary: RSC, Next-Router-State-Tree`,` x-matched-path: /cursos/[courseSlug]/[materialSlug]`, `Api-Gateway-Enabled: true`). The data bootstrap mechanism seems to have changed, so the prior `data = {}` pattern is gone.

Net result: Platzi pages under `/cursos/...` 200 OK, but the extractor can’t locate metadata/playlist data anymore and aborts with “Unable to extract data.”

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Log in to Platzi in the browser (active subscription).
2. Use browser cookies with yt-dlp.
3. Run:
```
PYTHONPATH=/home/<user>/Documents/Projects/yt-dlp \
YTDLP_NO_LAZY_EXTRACTORS=1 \
python3 -m yt_dlp -v --ignore-config \
  --use-extractors PlatziCourse \
  --write-pages --print-traffic \
  --add-header 'User-Agent:Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/118.0' \
  --cookies-from-browser brave:"<profile>" \
  'https://platzi.com/cursos/tecnologia-gerentes/el-mapa-de-un-gerente-en-tecnologia/'
```
## Expected Behavior

Extractor should parse course/lesson metadata and retrieve the list of video entries from the new /cursos pages, honoring authentication.

## Actual Behavior
Extractor errors with `RegexNotFoundError` for `data` and prints:
`ERROR: [PlatziCourse] ... Unable to extract data; please report this issue...`


### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: ScharadaValcta

title: [YouTube] subscription feed with `--flat-playlist` missing metadata fields
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

The short version is that sometime in the last week, `yt-dlp` with `--flat-playlist` is no longer successfully extracting channel information.

I noticed this issue on my `:ytsubs` scraping script, but I managed to reproduce it in a simpler way with a public playlist URL and no credentials.

In the output below, note especially `"channel_id": null, "channel": null, "channel_url": null, "uploader": null, "uploader_id": null, "uploader_url": null`.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

author: tianon

title: Youtube: Audio Format 140 Truncated on Recent Live Streams (YT-DLP Reports Success)
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Brazil

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

This issue affects all recent versions of yt-dlp when downloading audio in format 140 for Youtube videos.

Only the beginning of the audio is downloaded, yet the operation completes and is reported as successful. This behavior occurs in approximately 10% of the audio files I download from YouTube.


The download was done with this code:
```
from yt_dlp import YoutubeDL


def retrieve_audio_with_yt_dlp_v4(youtube_video_id, proxy_index, format_id, download_with_aria2c=True):

    if running_environment == 'LOCAL':
        quite_setting = False
    else:
        quite_setting = True
        
    proxy = proxy_handler_for_yt_dlp(proxy_index)

    video_url = f"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={youtube_video_id}"
    poToken = provider_of_potoken(youtube_video_id)

    ydl_opts = {

        # Path to your aria2c executable
        'external_downloader': ARIA2C_PATH_AND_FILE,
        # Any additional arguments
        'external_downloader_args': {
            'aria2c': ['-x', '16', '-s', '16', '-k', '1M']
        },


        'format': format_id,
        'proxy': proxy,  
        'outtmpl': f'cache/{youtube_video_id}.m4a',
        'quiet': quite_setting,
        'no_warnings': quite_setting,
        'noplaylist': True,
        'merge_output_format': None,  # don’t remux

        'writeinfojson': False,
        'writesubtitles': False,
        'writethumbnail': False,

        'extractor_args': {
            'youtube': [
                'player-client=web',
                f'po_token=web.player+{poToken}'
            ]
        }
    }
 


    with YoutubeDL(ydl_opts) as ydl:
        ydl.download([video_url])
```




Upon investigation, I found that all affected cases were related to videos that had been live-streamed recently. Most were streamed within the past few hours, though a few were streamed 10–20 hours prior.

Retrying after a few hours typically results in a successful download of the full audio.

Below is a list of recent examples. While all downloads were initiated using aria2c, the issue also occurs when using the native yt-dlp downloader, so it does not appear to be client-specific.


author: NannoSilver

title: [youtube] yt-dlp does not abort on 403 errors during `--live-from-start` download
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Hi, i am downloading a live webcam feed from the start with thousands of fragments. (<200k)

`yt-dlp.exe  --live-from-start --hls-use-mpegts -N 20 --fragment-retries 2 -o "%(title)s [%(id)s].%(ext)s" https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lJ6ApnNVHJY`

Download takes about 3-4h but it does not stop at the present time, it tries to enumerate way past the last fragments downloaded. Youtube is giving out 403's but it does not stop the download. I think there might be something wrong with the logic on how the end is detected in the --live-from-start switch.

DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

India

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

When attempting to download Hindi (`hi`) auto-generated subtitles for a YouTube Short using `yt-dlp`, I receive the error `ERROR: Did not get any data blocks`. The `--list-subs` command shows Hindi subtitles as available in multiple formats (VTT, TTML, SRV3, etc.), but downloading fails for all formats. This issue occurs consistently for the specific Short `https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7PehD76WRhg`.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run the following command to check available subtitles:
   ```bash
   yt-dlp --list-subs "https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7PehD76WRhg"
   ```
   - Output shows Hindi (`hi`) and other languages available in VTT, TTML, SRV3, SRV2, SRV1, JSON3 formats.
2. Attempt to download Hindi subtitles in VTT format:
   ```bash
   yt-dlp --write-subs --write-auto-subs --sub-lang hi --sub-format vtt --skip-download "https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7PehD76WRhg"
   ```
   - Result: `ERROR: Did not get any data blocks`.
3. Try other formats (e.g., TTML):
   ```bash
   yt-dlp --write-subs --write-auto-subs --sub-lang hi --sub-format ttml --skip-download "https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7PehD76WRhg"
   ```
   - Same error: `ERROR: Did not get any data blocks`.

## Expected Behavior
The command should download the Hindi subtitles as a `.vtt` or `.ttml` file (e.g., `How spectacles were invented 👓 [7PehD76WRhg].hi.vtt`), given that `--list-subs` indicates availability.

## Actual Behavior
The download fails with:
```
[info] Writing video subtitles to: How spectacles were invented 👓 [7PehD76WRhg].hi.vtt
ERROR: Did not get any data blocks
```

## Environment
- **yt-dlp version**: stable@2025.03.27 (from yt-dlp/yt-dlp, Debian package)
- **Python version**: 3.13.3 (CPython x86_64 64bit)
- **OS**: Ubuntu, Linux-6.14.0-29-generic-x86_64-with-glibc2.41
- **FFmpeg version**: 7.1.1
- **Optional libraries**: Cryptodome-3.20.0, brotli-1.1.0, certifi-2025.01.31, mutagen-1.47.0, pyxattr-0.8.1, requests-2.32.3, sqlite3-3.46.1, urllib3-2.3.0, websockets-14.1
- **Command-line config**: `--write-subs --write-auto-subs --sub-lang hi --sub-format vtt --skip-download --verbose`
- **Verbose log**: [Attached below](#verbose-log)

## Additional Context
- The YouTube Short’s URL is `https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7PehD76WRhg`.
- The verbose log mentions a missing GVS PO Token for iOS client formats, but this shouldn’t affect subtitles (tried `--extractor-args "youtube:formats=missing_pot"` with no success).
- The issue may be specific to YouTube Shorts or certain videos where auto-captions are listed but empty/inaccessible.
- Manual inspection in a browser (gear icon > Subtitles > Hindi) shows no subtitles displayed, suggesting YouTube’s auto-captions may be empty for this video.

## Proposed Solution
- Investigate why listed subtitles (e.g., Hindi in VTT) fail to download with `Did not get any data blocks`.
- Check if YouTube’s timedtext API (`https://www.youtube.com/api/timedtext?...`) is returning empty data for this Short.
- If this is a Shorts-specific issue, consider adding a warning in yt-dlp when subtitles are listed but empty.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: candrapersada

title: dir tree map/index/ToC?
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

The directory-tree structure of this repo seems "intuitive enough", but I've seen some files that are very big. Those files have good documentation (I appreciate that), but it's hard to "jump" to an area that's relevant to what I'm looking for. Is there a project map or something like that?

That was my "generic question", but my actual question is more specific. I want to write a stripped-down ("lite") implementation of YTDLP in another language, such that it has the following features:
- Can download individual media, as well as playlists
- Can extract audio from videos (`-x`)
- Only supports YT and SoundCloud
- Has basic formatting of output filenames. This is only useful when downloading playlists, because I want to include channel/author metadata.

That's it. If I ever need more features, I could implement them (or just install YTDLP). This is why I need a map, to search for the relevant code more effectively than using `grep -ri`.

The motivation for this "lite" variant is to make the CLI API simpler, and to minimize dependencies. Also, the only reason why I have Python installed on Termux is because of this package, and I want to avoid that, for several reasons

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)

### Region

Brazil

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

When a YouTube live hasn't started yet, `yt-dlp` errors out and prints `null`, even though basic metadata (title, channel, thumbnail, etc.) is available.

Steps to reproduce (replace the video ID with an upcoming live):
```shell
yt-dlp \
  --dump-single-json \
  --skip-download \
  --cookies /data/cookies.txt \
 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zW6uYRHgcUw
```
Result:
```
ERROR: [youtube] zW6uYRHgcUw: This live event will begin in a few moments.
null
```

Even if the livestream is not yet live, `yt-dlp` should return available metadata in JSON (title, channel/author, thumbnails, scheduled start time/status if known etc.) or provide a flag to allow "metadata-only" output for upcoming lives without failing the command. The `oembed` endpoint returns several fields for the same video ID before the live begins:

```shell
VIDEO_ID="zW6uYRHgcUw"
JSON_URL="https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=${VIDEO_ID}&format=json"
wget -q -O - "$JSON_URL" | jq
```
Result:
```json
{
  "title": "AO VIVO: Julgamento de Bolsonaro no STF hoje terá voto de Cármen Lúcia",
  "author_name": "BBC News Brasil",
  "author_url": "https://www.youtube.com/@BBCNewsBrasil",
  "type": "video",
  "height": 150,
  "width": 200,
  "version": "1.0",
  "provider_name": "YouTube",
  "provider_url": "https://www.youtube.com/",
  "thumbnail_height": 360,
  "thumbnail_width": 480,
  "thumbnail_url": "https://i.ytimg.com/vi/zW6uYRHgcUw/hqdefault.jpg",
  "html": "<iframe width=\"200\" height=\"150\" src=\"https://www.youtube.com/embed/zW6uYRHgcUw?feature=oembed\" frameborder=\"0\" allow=\"accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share\" referrerpolicy=\"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\" allowfullscreen title=\"AO VIVO: Julgamento de Bolsonaro no STF hoje terá voto de Cármen Lúcia\"></iframe>"
}
```

Currently for my python setup the following options is being used


title: [ie/smotrim] The problem of parsing a different type of embedded player on 'channel' URL
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site

DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Russia

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I want to supplement the recently released commit [[ie/smotrim] Rework extractors #14200](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/14200) with the following on the page [https://smotrim.ru/channel/270](https://smotrim.ru/channel/270) a different type of embedded player and iframe source is used, so getting the m3u8 list doesn't work. 
I found that the desired address of the embedded player's page is contained in the JSON-LD schema in the 'embedUrl' key and can be obtained with the following code:
```python
json_ld = self._parse_json(
  self._search_regex(
    JSON_LD_RE, webpage, 'JSON-LD', '{}', group='json_ld'),
  display_id, fatal=False)
src_url = traverse_obj(json_ld, ('embedUrl', {url_or_none}))
```
Next, the 'hls' and 'mpd' streams sources are obtained from the contents of the <script> inside the <head> section, which contains a dictionary with a calculated address to a json script ([https://media.mediavitrina.ru/balancer/v3/1tv/1tvch/streams.json?application_id=&player_referer_hostname=smotrim.ru&config_checksum_sha256=&egress_version_id=5942287&](https://media.mediavitrina.ru/balancer/v3/1tv/1tvch/streams.json?application_id=&player_referer_hostname=smotrim.ru&config_checksum_sha256=&egress_version_id=5942287&)) containing the source addresses on keys: `'hls'`, `'hlsp'`, `'mpdp'`.

**I think it's premature to add a commit #14200 to the release.**

title: [GetCourseRu] kalachevaschool.ru: unable to extract title
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Russia, Netherlands 

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

http://kalachevaschool.ru which is actually yet another one GetCourseRu-based site stopped giving *correct* playlists after my session of successful downloading with the following  warning:

```
WARNING: [GetCourseRu] unable to extract title; please report this issue on  https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q= , filling out the appropriate issue template. Confirm you are on the latest version using  yt-dlp -U
```

I usually use YoutubeDL in Jupiter Notebook, manually extracting masterPlaylistUrl using `gecodriver` and `webdriver.Firefox` like this:

```python
...
    driver.get('https://kalachevaschool.ru/teach/control/lesson/view/id/176422879')    
        
    video_iframes = driver.find_elements(By.CLASS_NAME, ITEM_IFRAME_PLAYER_CLS)
    print('frames:', len(video_iframes))
    
    for idx, iframe in enumerate(video_iframes, start=1):
        driver.switch_to.default_content()
        driver.switch_to.frame(iframe)

        video = driver.find_element(By.CLASS_NAME, 'vpl-root')
        script = driver.find_element(By.TAG_NAME, ITEM_HTML_ELEMENT).get_attribute('innerHTML')

        playlist = re.search(r'"masterPlaylistUrl":"([^"]+)"', script).groups()[0].replace(r'\/', '/')
        print(playlist)

        out_file_tpl = (
            f'{OUTPUT_FOLDER}'
            f'/{NAME_PREFIX} — s{parent.idx:02d} — {parent.header}'
            f'/{NAME_PREFIX} — s{parent.idx:02d}e{lesson.idx:02d}{idx:02d} '
            f'- {lesson.header}.%(ext)s'
        )
        print('out_file: ', out_file_tpl)
        
        opts = {
            'format': 'bv*[height<=720]',
            'nocheckcertificate': True,
            'outtmpl': out_file_tpl,
            'concurrent-fragments': 4,
            'download_archive': DOWNLOAD_ARCHIVE}
    
        with YoutubeDL(opts) as ydl:
            ydl.download([playlist])
...
```

(Actually I didn't know that yt-dlp can get videolinks from a page on its own, lol. RTFM.)

When I started getting errors yesterday, I switched to searching for playlists using YouTube itself:

```python
    driver.get('https://kalachevaschool.ru/teach/control/lesson/view/id/176422879')
    
    out_file_tpl = (
        f'{OUTPUT_FOLDER}'
        f'/{NAME_PREFIX} — s{parent.idx:02d} — {parent.header}'
        f'/{NAME_PREFIX} — s{parent.idx:02d}e{lesson.idx:02d}%(playlist_index)s '
        f'- {lesson.header}.%(ext)s'
    )
    print('out_file: ', out_file_tpl)
    
    opts = {
        'format': 'bv*[height<=720]',
        'nocheckcertificate': True,
        'outtmpl': out_file_tpl,
        'concurrent-fragments': 4,
        'download_archive': DOWNLOAD_ARCHIVE,
        'verbose': True}

    with YoutubeDL(opts) as ydl:
        ydl.download([playlist])
```

Naturally, it didn't help. Well, I manually extracted the playlist address, made sure the file downloaded and opened successfully:

author: dmlogv

title: [youtube] question about player PO tokens with the web client
body: ### Checklist - [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site - [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels)) - [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates ### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood This concerns an issue in cli_to_api.py, which appears to be incorrectly translating CLI arguments into API/library parameters related to potoken. The file is available here: `https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/devscripts/cli_to_api.py` You can find the potoken-related commands [here](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/PO-Token-Guide). It specifies the following arguments for using potoken: `--extractor-args "youtube:player-client=default,mweb;po_token=mweb.player+PO_TOKEN_VALUE_HERE"` The `https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/devscripts/cli_to_api.py` translates the above arguments (modified to `web` client) to this API (library) options: ``` {'extractor_args': {'youtube': {'player_client': ['web'], 'po_token': ['web.player+PO_TOKEN_VALUE_HERE']}}} ``` The above translation was implemented below and executed using YT-DLP version 2025.09.05 with Python 3.12, which shows the error: ``` youtube_video_id = 'rc7zW2u6E-g' format_id = '140' video_url = f"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={youtube_video_id}" # add your own potoken here: poToken = 'MpoBFwfyaW7R4m8XOXzimw4bmvF-02yvfOPDcgHuUOzbDBTXnyjaADLboGL5rviAtZzooqIXa_3dUfmsYya5lN-Y5_bu_x_rTVBWRZT2irgFkmQARtoweyJASPyvzdvWe_JddiBD1hHhy94HTKJPZa8dRwEqiTaK1HZA9ztLGdhdT0kFaLeFsmhF6hjVgCgbZ7BbW7DAJFkSvSKerg==' ydl_opts = { 'format': format_id, 'outtmpl': f'{youtube_video_id}.m4a', 'quiet': False, 'no_warnings': False, 'noplaylist': True, 'merge_output_format': None, # don’t remux 'verbose': True, 'player_client': 'web', # include or exclude this options does not change the outcome 'writeinfojson': False, 'writesubtitles': False, 'writethumbnail': False, 'extractor_args': {'youtube': {'player_client': ['web'], 'po_token': [f'web.player+{poToken}']}} } with YoutubeDL(ydl_opts) as ydl: ydl.download([video_url]) ``` It fails with the error: ``` [debug] Encodings: locale cp1252, fs utf-8, pref cp1252, out utf-8 (No ANSI), error utf-8 (No ANSI), screen utf-8 (No ANSI) [debug] yt-dlp version stable@2025.09.05 from yt-dlp/yt-dlp [50136eeeb] (pip) API [debug] params: {'format': If, instead, the potoken is specified in a different way, as `'extractor_args': {'youtube': [f'po_token=web.player+{poToken}']}`, the download is completed with success, but I am not sure if the author: k3vin-huy

title: Feature: Filter: Bestaudio For Each Language (all language audio option)
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Hi, I just noticed a usability quirk related to multiple audio streams in different languages.
For archival purposes you really want something like bestvideo+bestaudio but for each language (bestaudio only selects one stream). However this does not seem to be possible with the current format filter or option syntax. I think this is because the language is like a separate, orthogonal dimension to all the other filters while "normal" filters act on a one dimensional set which eventually reduces to a single format.

Is there some (combination of) option(s) I'm missing to make this work?
At the moment there are ~83 supported languages for the youtube extractor, so using option 3 below is practically infeasible after more than a couple of languages of interest because of the sheer amount of possible combinations.
I guess it is possible to use some ` --exec` post-processing hackery to make that work, however this use case seems like it should be simple to accomplish without external scripts.

If this is not possible please consider the feature request below.
I think my preference would be option C because of the orthogonality issue mentioned above.

(No output given because this is strictly a feature request)

## Feature description
 * Ability to download bestvideo+bestaudio for each language dub and merge them all into a single file.
 * Same behavior for video if there are language specific video streams.

## Things I've tried
(`--audio-multistreams` is enabled)
 1. `--format bestvideo*+mergeall[vcodec=none]` Over-selects ALL audio streams, even if multiple (worse) streams of the same language are present (I only want the best for each unique language)
 2. `--format bestvideo*+mergeall[acodec!=none]` Fetches way too many video streams so I aborted the download (Other than that should be same as 1).
 3. `--format bestvideo*+bestaudio[language=en]+bestaudio[language=de] #... listing every language of interest manually` Fails even if one language is missing and falls back to the next selection in the `/` list. Infeasible to list every possible combination

## Resolution Options
  * A. Special case language filter `all` to expand the filter to all laguages. e.g.: `--format bv+bestaudio[language=all]` would expand to `bv+bestaudio[language=X]+bestaudio[language=Y]...` and so on, for each language the extractor can find.
  * B. Special case filter akin to `mergeall` like `mergealllanguages`
  * C. A New option like `--sub-langs all` to handle multistream audio/video e.g. `--audio-langs all` and `--video-langs all`

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell

```
author: Robotic-Brain

title: Auto-detect and download the original-language subtitle without explicitly specifying the language code
body:
I want to download the subtitle that is already in the video’s original language (i.e. the language the video was uploaded with) without having to guess or specify the language code manually.
Today I have to do something like
yt-dlp --write-subs --sub-lang en --skip-download URL
and hard-code “en” (or any other code).
If the uploader used a different language I first need to run --list-subs, find the correct code and run the command again.
Could we have a convenience option (e.g. --sub-lang orig, --sub-lang auto, or a new switch --write-auto-sub) that simply picks the subtitle track whose language tag matches the video’s original language as returned by YouTube?

Russia

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/14200 if earlier only the http protocol did not work and m3u8 worked, then after this change nothing works, in the versions before the change both receiving links and downloading via m3u8 works

body:

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))

- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

On nicovideo.jp channels like https://www.nicovideo.jp/user/804800, while it's retrieving JSON Metadata pages if it receives the wrong amount of videos it will loop indefinitely (This channel reports to have 121 videos, but only 120 are returned) the extractor gets stuck saying 'Downloading JSON metadata page N'
using --lazy-playlist does download videos but still gets stuck

title: --max_downloads does not work as expected with cumulative downloads
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Hi, 

I resumed a download that had been cancelled previously. >1k files were already downloaded., now pursuing a more subtle strategy to avoid problems.

I was using the following script.

yt-dlp.exe -U --cookies-from-browser opera --check-formats -f "ba/b" --sleep-interval 240 --max-downloads 10 --no-mtime -x --audio-format mp3 --audio-quality 2 -o ".\dl%%(uploader)s%%(title)s.%%(ext)s" "%1"

My expectations are 240s breaks between downloads, and no more than 10 downloads at a time.

8 out of 10 downloads were skipped in this way (already in format...), as was expected. 2 files got effectively downloaded. However my expectation was that up to 10 files would be downloaded due to --max_downloads. Instead, all downloads were halted after the 10th item, regardless of whether a download took place.

I will add verbose output when I can, output below is not verbose sorry.
