author: NovaDevCo

title: Cache previous request metadata (at least for `-F`)
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I would like to have an ability to cache some meta information between each command running for a short time to decrease request counts and save my time.

For example, I run a command to get available formats with `-F`.
Next, I run a command to download the required formats with `-f`, but the program performs all download actions from zero, while it could reuse the metadata from previous running and start to download the content instantly.

I think using `-F` before `-f` (or without) is a pretty common use case and adding a cache optimization for it makes sense.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell

```

author: CaramelConnoisseur

title: [ie/smotrim] add information to fields
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a site-specific feature
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

russia

### Example URLs

https://smotrim.ru/video/2995947

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

https://a.i.getapic.me/n9sa.png 
currently the resolution fields are unknown, but they can be filled in based on the name of the streams

some gui and program plugins (for example for download master, as found out here https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/9372#issuecomment-1979090139 ) request a resolution and if it is unknown, then they download only the maximum size, and it doesn't matter what you choose in the settings 

because it asks for format **-g -e --all-formats --get-format** and there should be a resolution but there isn't and the DM plugin looks like it takes it from that field

I saw a couple more guis where the resolution selection buttons were also unavailable due to an unknown resolution field
maybe at least here, in this extractor, add resolution to these fields? here the streams are signed by bitrate, but as an exception, you can add an approximate resolution? well or somehow else determine the resolution, maybe there is more data somewhere on the site

https://a.i.getapic.me/n9su.png well or in the same json it is written the resolution on the links to mp4, which are not available, maybe you can get the resolution by these keys?

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

title: Youtube Audio "Error parsing Opus packet header" with ffmpeg 8.0
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

`yt-dlp --extract-audio -f bestaudio --keep-video -vU <any video url with opus>`

After downloading audio from YT, ffmpeg gives an error for the webm file, but the original Opus file seems ok.
I am not sure if this is a problem with ffmpeg or yt-dlp, but it only happens for YT audio with yt-dlp.

If analysed with
`ffmpeg -v warning -i <filename> -f null - 2>&1`

the *.opus file shows no errors, but the *.webm file always shows
`[opus @ 00000124624d8bc0] Error parsing Opus packet header.`
where the address changes with each call.

Any help is much appreciated, I mainly just try to understand what is happening here.


### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a new site support request

- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Region

Worldwide

### Example URLs

-Playlist for the entire course: https://lms.pilotinstitute.com/class/87
-Single video: https://lms.pilotinstitute.com/class/87/lesson/5670

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

You create a free account and can then stream video. I'm not sure how they authenticate the process to download video but in the inspect element there is a playlist.m3u8 which has the video info. I've been able to use mpv to stream playlist.m3u8 in inspect element. The site does track how much you've viewed the video but that isn't necessary for the extractor.

The extractor would make it easier to review watched videos offline without going through the hassle of opening the site which is very slow

title: -o option ignored when downloading subtitles with --convert-subs (filename suffix still appended)
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

### **Description**

When downloading subtitles with `--write-subs` (or `--write-auto-subs`) and `--convert-subs srt`, the `-o` option is not respected.  

Even if a fixed filename is provided, yt-dlp still appends the language code and original extension, producing an unexpected filename. This makes scripting unreliable since the resulting file cannot be predicted.  

**Example command:**

```bash
yt-dlp --write-subs --sub-lang "en-nP7-2PuUl7o" --convert-subs srt --skip-download -o "temp_sub.srt" "https://youtu.be/AmEIemQfk34?si=lhBUh8QXrBpnoW80"


### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

### Region

Globally

### Example URLs

Links are in the format `https://xhamsterlive.com/{username}`, however valid URLs for capture will change change by the minute as streamers come online and go offline, to get current usable URLs for development:
 
1. Go to https://xhamsterlive.com/
2. Pick any currently live stream
3. Confirm it loads and starts streaming - if a streamer is having connectivity issues some streams get stuck on a spinning circle of dots.

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Watching the Network tab of DevTools, it appears the required m3u8 and mp4 links changes frequently during the live stream (I think every second), additional logic will be need to detect and gracefully handle this as the page's JavaScript does.

title: Just `--write-video-urls tofile.txt` and then exit so I can run downloads my way, or do a resumable batch
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Sometimes you come across an unusually large playlist.  Some youtube channels for example have a video count in the 10000 range.

`--sleep-request` applies both to retrieving the playlist and the videos themselves.  This means if you need to stop and resume getting this huge list for any reason you will need to wait the same amount of time for ytdl to paginate through the playlist as the politeness you want to use for getting videos.  It seem currently impossible to just write out the urls that came out of the playlist without also going through the usual extractor steps to get the whole info json.  However it is clearly enumerating all of the urls upfront.

Having the ability to dump the url list instead of just getting it into memory and then processing it gives much greater flexibility to split enumerating videos and getting videos when systems pupeting ytdl need to get a large list of videos over a long period of time and should be able to recover from ytdl exiting for any reason.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell
Not applicable this is requesting a non existing feature.
```

title: Even when using the recommended Get cookies.txt LOCALLY, I get the error "http.cookiejar.LoadError: 'cookies.txt' does not look like a Netscape format cookies file"
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))

DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I also used Get-cookies.txt-LOCALLY to export cookies in Netscape format, but I still get the error “does not look like a Netscape format cookies file”.
I tried the latest builds of stable, nightly, and master, but the same error occurs.
I also get the error even when using the latest FFmpeg-Builds for yt-dlp.
I also get the error when using the latest PowerShell.
I confirmed that the first line contains # Netscape HTTP Cookie File.
I saw issue #13509, but since I am not running on a server, I don’t think it is related.

However, there is only one method that worked:
When I typed cmd in Explorer from the directory where yt-dlp.exe is located and ran yt-dlp from there, the error did not occur.

What could be causing this error?

title: Cannot download bilibili danmu with aria2c
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

Using the native downloader on bilibili occassionally [throttles](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/10849) my download speed, so I have use aria2c to download on bilibili. However, when downloading danmu/danmaku using aria2c, aria2c fails to download (which isn't a `yt-dlp` issue I believe, as trying to use aria2c to directly download the danmu `.xml` gives the same error)

[Downloading danmu directly with aria2c log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22393536/log.txt)

I'm trying to embed the danmu into the video using [this plugin](https://github.com/UlyssesZh/yt-dlp-danmaku). I understand that I can first download the actual video/audio content with aria2c, then run the same command without `--downloader aria2c` but with `--embed-sub --use-postprocessor danmaku --sub-lang danmaku` in order to embed the danmu, but this is rather cumbersome.

[Example log of what I'm talking about.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22393756/Example.txt)

What other fixes can I try and/or can I specify a downloader only for the video/audio download, and not have it be applied for the danmu?

author: InfoLibre

title: ffmpeg not working on Android
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I can not use ffmpeg on android (15):

I tried using

 /data/data/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/ffmpeg
/data/user/0/com.flet.src/cache/ffmpeg
/data/data/com.flet.src/files/ffmpeg

as paths from where yt-dlp should fetch ffmpeg to convert .m4a to .mp3.

Sucesfully ran "chmod(0o755)" and ffmpeg is available and executable.
i have a debug dialog inside the app and a logger  to debug whats going on. and no matter what i do i always end up with:

ERROR: Postprocessing: ffprobe and ffmpeg not found. Please install or provide the path using ffmpeg-location



even though debug info says:

Working Directory: /data/data/com.flet.src/files/flet/app
file (main): /data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/main_view.py
FLET_PLATFORM: android

ffmpeg search:
✓ /data/data/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/ffmpeg (size=50384400 bytes)
✗ /data/data/com.flet.src/files/flet/src/ffmpeg
✗ /data/data/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/src/ffmpeg
✓ /data/data/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/ffmpeg (size=50384400 bytes)
✓ /data/user/0/com.flet.src/cache/ffmpeg (size=50384400 bytes)

ffmpeg executable: True
ffprobe executable: True




relevant code:

def _ffmpeg_localer() -> str | None:
    m = platform.machine().lower()
    if "x86_64" in m or "amd64" in m:
        return "/usr/bin/ffmpeg"

    # ARM64: ffmpeg aus Assets kopieren
    if "aarch64" in m or "arm64" in m:
        exec_dir = Path("/data/data/com.flet.src/files/")

        for binary_name in ["ffmpeg", "ffprobe"]:

            dst = exec_dir / binary_name
            try:
                if not dst.exists():
                    shutil.copy2(binary_name, dst)
                    dst.chmod(0o755)

            except Exception as e:
                return None

        return str(exec_dir)

    return None


def download(
    info: dict,
    out_path: Path,
    progress_cb: Optional[Callable[[dict], None]] = None,
    is_canceled: Optional[Callable[[], bool]] = None,
    log: Optional[object] = None
) -> None:

    url = info.get("webpage_url")
    if not url:
        raise ValueError("download_service: URL missing")

    out_path = Path(out_path)
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    outtmpl = str(out_path.parent / out_path.stem)

    def _hook(d: dict):
        if is_canceled and is_canceled():
            raise yt_dlp.utils.DownloadError("Download manuell abgebrochen")

        if progress_cb:
            try:
                progress_cb(d)
            except Exception:
                pass


    ydl_opts_arm64 = {
        "format": "bestaudio/best",
        "outtmpl": outtmpl,
        "noplaylist": True,
        "progress_hooks": [_hook],
        "ffmpeg_location": _ffmpeg_localer(), #Has been set to various locations as variables, Paths, Strings...
        "logger": log,
        "verbose": True,
        "postprocessors": [
            {
                "key": "FFmpegExtractAudio",
                "preferredcodec": "mp3",
                "preferredquality": "0",
            }
        ],
    }

.....



ABOUT THE VERBOSE LOG:

I can only provide a snipped of the verbose output because flet doesnt let you select the text in a row on android. i captured the relevant part with google lens to copy/paste it as text. (the way my logger works does'nt allow the flag 'selectable'. will be fixed in the future) 



You can try it yourself, i have the app on my github page:
(sorry for the messed up readme)
https://github.com/GENOK9/easy_soundcloud_download
It works perfectly fine on Desktop linux.




System info:
Flet Version: 0.28.3
Python Version: 3.13.5
yt-dlp-2025.9.17.232802.dev0

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell
ERROR: ERROR: Postprocessing: ffprobe and ffmpeg not found. Please install or provide the path using-ffmpeg-location

ERROR: Traceback (most recent call last):

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py", line 3612, in process_info

replace_info_dict(self.post_proce ss(dl_filename, info_dict, files_to_move))

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py", line 3798, in post_process info =

self.run_all_pps('post_process', info, additional_pps=info.get('_pos tprocessors'))

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py", line 3780, in run_all_pps

info = self.run_pp(pp, info)

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py", line 3758, in run_pp files_to_delete, infodict = pp.run(infodict)

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/postprocessor/common.py", line 23, in run

ret = func(self, info, *args, **kwargs)

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/postprocessor/common.py", line 128, in wrapper

return func(self, info)

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/postprocessor/ffmpeg.py", line 493, in run filecodec =

self.get_audio_codec(path)

File "/data/user/0/com.flet.src/files/flet/app/yt_dlp/postprocessor/ffmpeg.py", line 241 in get_audio_codec raise

author: GENOK9

title: Some youtube videos successfully downloading, but Error opening files is raised
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

United Kingdom

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

When trying to download videos, some (examples: Stephen Colbert and Seth Meyers and Failarmy), but not all, successfully download, but fail to open/be muxed. The below is typical in all failure cases in multiple resolutions.

Downloading happens, files exist, can't be merged.

title: How to make yt-dlp print results from --dump-json chunk by chunk on really large playlists
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

I am using --flat-playlist
If we are querying large playlists 1000+ it takes a considerable amount of time to finish.
There are commands like --playlist-items but judging by the fact that the performance gets worse with each iteration
Im assuing that yt-dlp is querying youtube playlists by pages and loading each one of them until it reaches my index.

The most efficient way would be to run one command and as it parses youtube pages, it prints out the 100 video chunk it got from the page in the output stream, instead of printing in the end.

Can this be done?

Below im just using playlist-items 1-1 for example purposes

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)

- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: bjarnerev-sketch

title: How to get a list of all playlists
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

I use this command to get a json list of my Watch Later list:
```
yt-dlp --cookies-from-browser chrome --dump-json --flat-playlist 'https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=WL' | tee -a youtube_watch_later_list_$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M).json
```
Is there a way to do the same for ALL my playlists (including private playlists)?

Or a way to just get the links to all my playlists, so I could use xargs to then get the json list of all those?
author: drelephant

title: Bitdefender goes nuts and deletes everything that is open with no recovery
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))

DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Are you guys aware that Bitdefender goes absolutely nuts every once in a while from your application? It will literally delete everything that is running on Windows 10 and there is no way to restore the files. This has happened three times now over the last year. I have lost lots of important data and I have no way to recover it. Using the restore button does not restore anything. I know this is not a you thing and it is a Bitdefender thing, but your application is literally the only one that causes it to get triggered. Could you please not program your app with a function that triggers one of the biggest antivirus apps to delete my computer.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

Seriously.

author: ghyujkilop

title: ApplePodcasts not working again
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

i am trying to download a podcast from applepodcasts. This was working up at least the first week of September. It is now claiming that no video formats can be found. Happy to provide more information if required

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell

author: vdv148

title: Getting the url without redirection
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

I come accross hls streams on various sites and I want to ommit the quality parameter when getting the url with yt-dlp so as to let the player (mpv below, kodi on other system) pick the right resolution depending on the available bandwidth.
It is simple with youtube-dl because this bit
```
$ youtube-dl --get-url https://www.star.gr/tv/psychagogia/farma/1592025-farma-premiera
WARNING: Falling back on generic information extractor.
https://deliver2.siliconweb.com/kwik/star.m3u8?key=Esz6I15mDo84TWaTcfyuA
```
produces the url that is hls compatible, so when it is played with mpv, mpv auto selects the resolution given the available bandwidth (limited to 1.5 Mbps with `hls-bitrate=1500000` via config file for demonstration)
```
$ mpv https://deliver2.siliconweb.com/kwik/star.m3u8?key=Esz6I15mDo84TWaTcfyuA
● Video  --vid=1  (h264 854x480 25 fps 1117 kbps)
○ Video  --vid=2  (h264 1280x720 25 fps 1914 kbps)
○ Video  --vid=3  (h264 1920x1080 25 fps 2609 kbps)
● Audio  --aid=1  (aac 2ch 48000 Hz 1117 kbps)
○ Audio  --aid=2  (aac 2ch 48000 Hz 1914 kbps)
○ Audio  --aid=3  (aac 2ch 48000 Hz 2609 kbps)
(pointless ffmpeg errors removed)
AO: [alsa] 48000Hz stereo 2ch float
VO: [gpu] 854x480 => 854x480 vaapi[nv12]
Now playing: star.m3u8?key=Esz6I15mDo84TWaTcfyuA
AV: 00:00:00 / 02:33:37 (0%) A-V: -0.008 Cache: 12s/1MB  
```
Yt-dlp though selects the best (or configured quality, 720p in that case), because it follows the "redirection" to the needed stream
```
$ yt-dlp --get-url https://www.star.gr/tv/psychagogia/farma/1592025-farma-premiera
WARNING: [generic] Falling back on generic information extractor
https://starmotionvod.siliconweb.com/starvod/smil:itwfcdn/starmotionvod/2432158-bp6461BEF2GL8J3.smil/index-f2-v1-a1.m3u8

$ mpv https://starmotionvod.siliconweb.com/starvod/smil:itwfcdn/starmotionvod/2432158-bp6461BEF2GL8J3.smil/index-f2-v1-a1.m3u8 
● Video  --vid=1  (h264 1280x720 25 fps)
● Audio  --aid=1  (aac 2ch 48000 Hz)
Using hardware decoding (vaapi).
AO: [alsa] 48000Hz stereo 2ch float
VO: [gpu] 1280x720 vaapi[nv12]
Now playing: index-f2-v1-a1.m3u8
AV: 00:00:01 / 02:33:37 (0%) A-V: -0.000 Cache: 10s/2MB   
```
Curl output for redirection proof
```
$ curl -L "https://deliver2.siliconweb.com/kwik/star.m3u8?key=Esz6I15mDo84TWaTcfyuA"
#EXTM3U
#EXT-X-STREAM-INF:PROGRAM-ID=1,BANDWIDTH=1117335,RESOLUTION=854x480,FRAME-RATE=25.000,CODECS="avc1.64001e,mp4a.40.2",VIDEO-RANGE=SDR,CLOSED-CAPTIONS=NONE
index-f1-v1-a1.m3u8
#EXT-X-STREAM-INF:PROGRAM-ID=1,BANDWIDTH=1913939,RESOLUTION=1280x720,FRAME-RATE=25.000,CODECS="avc1.64001f,mp4a.40.2",VIDEO-RANGE=SDR,CLOSED-CAPTIONS=NONE
index-f2-v1-a1.m3u8
#EXT-X-STREAM-INF:PROGRAM-ID=1,BANDWIDTH=2608500,RESOLUTION=1920x1080,FRAME-RATE=25.000,CODECS="avc1.640028,mp4a.40.2",VIDEO-RANGE=SDR,CLOSED-CAPTIONS=NONE
index-f3-v1-a1.m3u8

#EXT-X-I-FRAME-STREAM-INF:BANDWIDTH=319302,RESOLUTION=854x480,CODECS="avc1.64001e",URI="iframes-f1-v1-a1.m3u8",VIDEO-RANGE=SDR
#EXT-X-I-FRAME-STREAM-INF:BANDWIDTH=525827,RESOLUTION=1280x720,CODECS="avc1.64001f",URI="iframes-f2-v1-a1.m3u8",VIDEO-RANGE=SDR
#EXT-X-I-FRAME-STREAM-INF:BANDWIDTH=694406,RESOLUTION=1920x1080,CODECS="avc1.640028",URI="iframes-f3-v1-a1.m3u8",VIDEO-RANGE=SDR
```
How can I avoid that redirection and get an hls compatible url? To be exact, I want to get either the above url or, ideally, the one mentioned below, on the line that starts with `[redirect]`.

p.s. The forementioned site was intentionally used so as to show what I want with youtube-dl. I could use twitch or any other site that uses hls for streaming, but youtube-dl would give the same output as yt-dlp.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: pitsi

title: Feature request to accept formats with dash via the -f option
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a site-specific feature
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Example URLs

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O-3gEsfEm0g

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Sometimes the audio formats are listed with variants that specify a dash, e.g. `251-9'. However, the `-f` option doesn't seem to accept these:

```
$ yt-dlp -f 242+251-9 'https://youtu.be/O-3gEsfEm0g'
[youtube] Extracting URL: https://youtu.be/O-3gEsfEm0g
[youtube] O-3gEsfEm0g: Downloading webpage
[youtube] O-3gEsfEm0g: Downloading tv simply player API JSON
[youtube] O-3gEsfEm0g: Downloading tv client config
[youtube] O-3gEsfEm0g: Downloading tv player API JSON
ERROR: [youtube] O-3gEsfEm0g: Requested format is not available. Use --list-formats for a list of available formats
```
Instead, I can only use `-f 242+251` but that doesn't allow me to easily pick any of the actual choices listed for `-F`. I assume there is another way to do this, which is fine, but perhaps `-f` could be expanded so it also directly understands these new dash formats?

My apologies if there is a duplicate for this, I tried to find one but the Github search only presents me result listings with multiple dozens of pages of entries no matter what related search keywords I try, so I have no idea if this was already filed or not.

author: Cryosim

title: `--match-filter` not doing what's expected when it includes references to `playlist_uploader_id`
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I was trying to download from [this YouTube playlist](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL4Mh08dMZMVyU_x5LD6sexZFAzrmB7Zmi) as a test.  I only wanted videos in the playlist where `playlist_uploader_id` matched `uploader_id`.  But even though the output from `--print playlist_uploader_id` matched `--print uploader_id`, `--match-filter 'playlist_uploader_id=uploader_id'` fails.  In fact, for me, it only tests once before quiting and doesn't list what it rejected.  (I have samples below.)  I also tried it with `--print pre_process:...` in an attempt to see what it was actually comparing, but that had no effect.

My first post in Discord was https://discord.com/channels/807245652072857610/807247901981016094/1418603289121132654.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

author: WillPittenger

title: --no-restrict-filenames no longer honored
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature

author: XtremeBear

title: Ignore --list-formats when dumping JSON
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Currently `yt-dlp` still prints the list of formats to the console when `--dump-json` or `--dump-single-json` options are used for programmatic usage.

This can cause issues if `--list-formats` or any other information printing options are specified in the config file.

A real world example of issue is present in mpv: https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv/pull/16800

I would suggest ignoring flags (so far I only know about `--list-formats`) which print anything to the console when JSON dumping is needed, this should avoid conflicts with programmatic usage.

author: DavidisanCHEATER

title: Download-sections used in Youtube creates ghost part of the video.
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

everywhere

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Using --download-sections flag when downloading a Youtube video creates a 10 second ghost section at the start of the downloaded video. it shows in the length of the video but there is no video there. (the additional 10 seconds are above the selected range so if Ex: "*15-25" is given, the video will be 20 seconds long with the first 10 seconds being blank)
I have tested different videos, different starting and ending points, different lengths, and it seems to be consistently 10 seconds. sometime last couple seconds of the additional 10 seconds are available.
Also tested Reddit which seems to work fine so i assume this is a youtube specific problem. 

Ex:



`yt-dlp  --download-sections "*00:01:40-00:02:00" "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pHvP71rwYAc"`
for this command here is the output 
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c7d5d9d7-9eba-452c-93d7-e997c1a105f7

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: ThDag

title: please fix the download of subtitle translate from english
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Italy

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I have tried everything to download Italian subtitles translated from English, which are available on any alternative frontend to YouTube. It used to work on ytdlp, but for the past three months I have been getting the same error message. I thought it was my fault and never reported it. I haven't downloaded anything for a month and I realized that something is broken in your program. Please fix it. I don't understand English at all, and subtitles are very important for me to understand what is being said in the video.
The error I get is this:
`ERROR: Unable to download video subtitles for 'it': HTTP Error 429: Too Many Requests`

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

author: dasaferi

title: Stop processing videos when videos are chronologically sorted, daterange is set and video date is beyond range
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

The `daterange` option is very helpful to reduce which videos get downloaded from a playlist, however every video page is still accessed and processed, which can take a long time for large playlists (hundreds of videos).

Many sites offer URLs which will return videos in chronological upload order.

Combining these I believe it should be possible to significantly improve processing time and reduce the risk of 429 Too Many Requests responses being triggered on rate-limited sites. In the example below, assuming a last 7 days daterange value, this could be reduced from fetching and processing 281 video pages to just 1 video page.

This could be implementing here with a new exception type (or string matching) to break from the processing loop: 

https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/bf5d18016b03a3f2fd5d3494d9efe85d3f8beeac/yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py#L1542-L1546

## Pseudo-code

```python
def _match_entry(self, info_dict, incomplete=False, silent=False, *, newest_first=None):
    # newest_first: Whether entires are chronologically sorted with the newest video first - set by IE.
    #   True = sorted newest to oldest
    #   False = sorted oldest to newest
    #   None (default) = not chronologically sorted

    def check_filter():

        # ...existing code...

        date = info_dict.get("upload_date")
        if date is not None:
            date_range = self.params.get("daterange", DateRange())

            if newest_first is None:
                # current logic
                if date not in date_range:
                    return f"{date_from_str(date).isoformat()} upload date is not in range {date_range}"
            else:
                if date in date_range:
                    # We want this video
                    pass
                elif newest_first and date > date_range.end:
                    # Videos are sorted newest to oldest and this one is older than we want.
                    # Therefore, this and all further videos are out of range.
                    raise OutOfDateRangeError(date_from_str(date).isoformat())
                elif not newest_first and date < date_range.start:
                    # Videos are sorted oldest to newest and this one is newer than we want.
                    # Therefore, this and all further videos are out of range.
                    raise OutOfDateRangeError(date_from_str(date).isoformat())
                else:
                    # We don't want this video, but may still want future ones.
                    return f"{date_from_str(date).isoformat()} upload date is not in range {date_range}"

        # ...existing code...
```

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

author: SwimmingLink

title: When downloading multiple streams fails, yt-dlp still merges them
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

When I download multiple streams such as 160+249 or whatever, and one stream fails to download for whatever reason (such as a bad connection or the stream not being available), yt-dlp will rename and merge whatever streams were successfully downloaded instead of letting them be with their original filenames. This means that if adjustments have to be made, such as a re-attempt or changing one of the streams to what is available, the app cannot do a proper merge but instead states the video has already been downloaded. If the merge is only attempted when ALL the streams are successfully downloaded, this would not be a problem. As it is, I have to rename the incomplete downloads then use ffmpeg manually to merge the streams which is a major pain the butt and would easily be avoided if the program didn't insist on trying to merge and rename a FAILED DOWNLOAD ATTEMPT.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

author: Fetch-user

title: Issue trying to add further support to CBC extractor
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

I am currently experimenting with adding support to the CBC extractor for items under cbc.ca/listen which the current extractor does not support.

When i load a page in my browser or using BeautifulSoup, for example:

https://www.cbc.ca/listen/cbc-podcasts/1353-the-naked-emperor/episode/16142603-introducing-understood-who-broke-the-internet

i can see the url i need to find within the json component of window.__PRELOADED_STATE__  in a script tag. 

`"url":"https://podcast-a.akamaihd.net/mp3/podcasts/nakedemperor/nakedemperor-lIEqHISf-20250425.mp3","clipID":16142603,`

However, when i load the page using _download_webpage in an extractor the json in __PRELOADED_STATE__ is incomplete and does not include the parts i need.

From what i have read this can happen if the site thinks you are a bot and so returns a "lite" version of the page.

I have tried a few things including:
-   using sanitized_Request to specify User-Agent and other items in the header 
`_BROWSER_HEADERS = {
        'User-Agent':
            'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) '
            'AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) '
            'Chrome/124.0.0.0 Safari/537.36',
        'Accept': 'application/json, text/plain, */*',
        'Referer': 'https://www.cbc.ca/listen/',
        'Origin':  'https://www.cbc.ca',
        'Cookie': 'cookie_consent=1',
    }
req = sanitized_Request(api_url, headers=_BROWSER_HEADERS)
raw = self._download_webpage(req, video_id)`
- an api i found a reference to that i think no longer exists as it returns a 404 error
`https://www.cbc.ca/listen/api/v1/episodes/{video_id}`

However i am still unable to get the full json that i need.

Any thoughts on if or how i can get the complete json returned?

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell

```
author: uoag

title: It seems like SABR is forced even with valid premium cookies and PO Token Provider
body: ### Checklist - [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature - [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme) - [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels)) - [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates ### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood I am unable to access premium formats on my premium account because YouTube seems to be forcing SABR streaming, even when providing valid cookies and using a PO Token provider. 1. Premium Account When I use my premium account, yt-dlp fails to list any premium formats and shows the SABR-only warning. This happens whether I use --cookies-from-browser or a --cookies file exported with an extension. Adult-restricted content can be downloaded, confirming that the login via cookies is successful. I have also set up and successfully run the `gutil-ytdlp-pot-provider. Log confirms that yt-dlp loads the plugin and that the server generates a token, but the SABR issue persists. 2. Non-Premium Account When I switch to a different, non-premium Google account, the SABR warning disappears, and yt-dlp works as expected (listing all available non-premium formats). This suggests my premium account has been flagged for SABR enforcement that the current PO Token provider cannot bypass. I would like to download 1080p premium(enhanced bitrate) video. Since yt-dlp doesn't show a premium option, what else could I do to download enhanced bitrate video? It is my first time using issues section, hope I don't violate any rules in here though. Thank you ### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem - [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU `) - [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead - title: SoundCloud: add a field to json output to tell if a track requires a Go+ subscription to download

### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Some tracks on SoundCloud can only be dowloaded/played with a Go+ subscription, such as this one: https://on.soundcloud.com/zm7BCOajdkkgYf1XVZ 

It would be nice if json output (`-J` parameter) included that information (that it requires Go+) to allow calling code to filter out such tracks.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

author: bubbleguuum

title: Inherit shell environment variables to improve scripting capabilities
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Shell environment variables are not inherited and this greatly limits scripting possibilities.

Example: I want to create a script that downloads a channel and use an archive file, but I want to have the archive options and the `-o` option in different config files loaded with the `--config-locations` option, these files are used by all scripts and must not be customized for a particular script.

- Create a shell script and add the `CHANNEL_NAME='Programming tutorials'` variable
- Invoke a configuration file for output options with `--config-locations "~/output-config"` which contains the following line
```
-o ~/Downloads/$CHANNEL_NAME/%(title)s.%(ext)s
```
- Invoke a configuration file for archive options with `--config-locations "~/archive-config"` which contains the following line
```
--download-archive "~/Downloads/$CHANNEL_NAME/archive.txt
```

When the config files are invoked, the variables are not interpreted and are treated literally. This strongly limits scripting capabilities.

Note: one might use `%(channel)s` instead but this means that if the channel changes name, for example from "Programming tutorials" to "Programming with John" (this happens very often with many channels), the download folder and the archive file both change, so the channel gets downloaded again.

This was just to provide an example scenario and I tried to keep things as simple as possible, but the possibilities are endless.

yt-dlp should always interpret variables in config files that are invoked to make scripting more flexible.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell

```

title: VP9 breaks remuxed clips
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))

- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

United Kingdom

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I'm running yt-dlp through the Homebrew formula on Linux, and when using `--compat-options prefer-vp9-sort`, `--remux-video mp4`, and `--download-sections` together, it creates a corrupted MP4 that makes VLC throw the error `VLC could not decode the format "vp09" (No description for this codec)`. It's been happening pretty consistently for every video - *except* after a restart, some videos that I had tried previously suddenly worked. I've attached the MP4 generated by the command I've included the log of, which came from the following command:

`yt-dlp -vU --cookies-from-browser firefox --remux-video mp4 -o "Output/%(title)s - %(id)s.%(ext)s"  --download-sections "*4:40-5:40" --compat-options prefer-vp9-sort "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NUHIAGnugJg"`

title: [XHamster] failed to decipher URL / No video formats found!
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Romania

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I think a new workaround is needed for xhamster.com. 😕
Deciphering of the URL's as introduced in #14286 doesn't seem to work any more.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

author: onlyjesuschristsaves

title: [Announcement] Upcoming new requirements for YouTube downloads
body:
### Beginning very soon, you'll need to have [Deno](https://deno.com/) (or another supported JavaScript runtime) installed to keep YouTube downloads working as normal.

## Why?

Up until now, yt-dlp has been able to use its [built-in JavaScript "interpreter"](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/2025.09.23/yt_dlp/jsinterp.py) to solve the JavaScript challenges that are required for YouTube downloads. But due to [recent changes on YouTube's end](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/14400), the built-in JS interpreter will soon be insufficient for this purpose. The changes are so drastic that [yt-dlp will need to leverage a proper JavaScript runtime in order to solve the JS challenges](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/14157).

## What do I need to do?

### Everyone will need to install [Deno](https://deno.com/) (or another supported JavaScript runtime; see the FAQ below).

yt-dlp will also need a few JavaScript components, and **this *may* require additional action from you** depending on how you installed yt-dlp:

- **Official PyInstaller-bundled executable users (e.g. `yt-dlp.exe`, `yt-dlp_macos`, `yt-dlp_linux`, etc) and zipimport binary users (the `yt-dlp` Unix executable)::**
    - No additional action required (besides having Deno or another supported JS runtime). All the necessary JavaScript components will be bundled with these executables.

- **PyPI package users (e.g. installed with `pip`, `pipx`, etc):**
    - Install *and upgrade* yt-dlp with the `default` optional dependency group included, e.g.: `pip install -U "yt-dlp[default]"`

- **Third-party package users (e.g. installed with `pacman`, `brew`, etc):**
    - The action required will depend on how your third-party package repository decides to handle this change. However, you will be able to run yt-dlp with the `--remote-components` option to download the necessary JS bundle from yt-dlp's GitHub release assets (or from NPM if you're using Deno).

---

# Frequently Asked Questions
(and answers to them)

1. ### Does this mean yt-dlp is unusable without Deno (or another supported JavaScript runtime)?

No. The JavaScript runtime requirement will only apply to downloading from YouTube. yt-dlp can still be used without it on [the other ~thousand sites it supports](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/2025.09.23/supportedsites.md).

The relationship between yt-dlp and the JS runtime will be analogous to the relationship between yt-dlp and ffmpeg. You need to bring your own ffmpeg, but yt-dlp can still do a lot of things without it.

Downloading *some* formats from YouTube without a JS runtime will still be possible for at least a little while longer: e.g. [`web_safari`](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#youtube) m3u8 formats do not require a `n` parameter nor do they require a [PO token](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/PO-Token-Guide). We'll try to keep these formats available for as long as possible.

2. ### Why can't yt-dlp's native JavaScript interpreter be improved to handle the new YouTube player JS?

Currently, yt-dlp relies on a complicated [set of regex patterns](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/4429fd0450a3fbd5e89573e06533c1a0874fae42/yt_dlp/extractor/youtube/_video.py#L2226-L2287) to identify the necessary functions in the YouTube player JS, and relies on even [more regex](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/yt_dlp/jsinterp.py) to try and interpret that JavaScript code natively within Python.

This code was already hugely costly to maintain. Any time YouTube changed something about their player JS, it would cause multiple maintainers to drop everything and spend all of their free time for the next 2-3 days trying to fix things.

With the latest changes, the necessary functions are spread out all over the player and are much more difficult to identify. Continuing to use the regex-based approach would be far too brittle and far too costly (i.e. maintainers would be spending all of their free time for the next 2-3 *weeks* after a breakage trying to fix things).

So the regex-based approach has been abandoned in favor of an AST-based approach (https://github.com/yt-dlp/ejs), which should allow us to respond much more quickly to changes in the player JS, even as its obfuscation increases.

3. ### Why *Deno*?

Deno has a highly restrictive permission model by default and does not allow filesystem or network access. This is essential to protect our less-technically-inclined users. It also comes as a single-file portable executable, making its "installation" comparable to that of ffmpeg (which is something that all of our users should already be familiar with.)

Another obstacle we are facing in the near future is [YouTube's proof-of-origin (PO) token](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/PO-Token-Guide) requirement being enforced for all YT player clients. Implementing a built-in solution to this will also require JavaScript functionality which far exceeds that of our native `jsinterp` module. So we chose a runtime that would be able to handle the JS challenges (`n` parameter/signature cipher) *as well as* PO token generation in the future.

4. ### Will other JavaScript runtimes be supported?

The [linked pull request](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/14157/files#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5) supports **Node**, **Bun**, and **QuickJS** in addition to Deno.

This was not advertised in the original announcement post because Deno is the safest option & we'd prefer less-technically-inclined yt-dlp users to stick with that, and Deno will be used by default while using Node/Bun/QuickJS will require user intervention (i.e. passing a CLI argument).

While Deno, Node, Bun, and QuickJS are the only runtimes that will be *initially* supported, we are open to adding support for more.

5. ### What about *PhantomJS*?

PhantomJS will no longer be used by yt-dlp's YouTube extractor in any capacity. The project is unmaintained, outdated and insecure. From a developer's perspective, it is nearly impossible to debug. There was an unsuccessful attempt made to get it working with this solution, but ultimately we decided we need to abandon it. For now, PhantomJS is still being used by a handful of other (non-YouTube) extractors.

6. ### What about *QuickJS*?

Edit: It will be supported.

7. ### What about *Selenium* or a headless browser solution?

The yt-dlp maintainers have no interest in doing this except as a measure of last resort. A headless browser solution would be an admission of defeat and would go against the spirit of this project.

8. ### Is this change the same thing as the [YTNSigDeno plugin](https://github.com/bashonly/yt-dlp-YTNSigDeno)?

No. The plugin just substitutes Deno for the native JSInterp after the `n` cipher function has already been extracted using Python regex. The actual solution will (out of necessity) completely delegate the `n` cipher function extraction to the JS runtime.

The plugin's development has been abandoned and the repository will be archived after yt-dlp has native Deno support.

9. ### What is the minimum required version of Deno?

- **Deno minimum required version**: `2.0.0`
    - *the latest version of Deno is strongly recommended*

10. ### What is the minimum required version of Node/Bun/QuickJS?

- **Node**: `20.0.0`
    - *the latest version of Node is strongly recommended for security reasons*
- **Bun**: `1.0.31`
    - *the latest version of Bun is strongly recommended*
- **QuickJS**: `2023-12-9`
    - *QuickJS version `2025-4-26` or later is strongly recommended for performance reasons*
- **QuickJS-ng**: *any*
    - *upstream QuickJS is strongly recommended instead for performance reasons*

11. ### What about platforms that aren't supported by the minimum required versions of these JavaScript runtimes?

We are open to adding support for other JavaScript runtimes so long as they fit into our established framework (see the [linked PR](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/14157)). Pull requests are welcome, but obviously we ask that you wait until the initial linked PR has been merged.

12. ### What do I need to do if I'm using \[*insert Linux/BSD distro here*\]?

You'll have to wait and see what your distro's package maintainers decide to do.

13. ### I installed Deno, but why doesn't yt-dlp seem to be using it?

This change has not yet been implemented. This announcement is intended to give everyone a heads up.
author: bashonly

title: Unsupported site causes an infinite loop of redirects
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

A website without an extractor, `arabsex.is`, causes an infinite loop of redirects and therefore a denial of service.

The log I'm attaching is truncated - I pressed ^C.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

author: Jsobo217

title: FFmpeg downloader must verify TLS certificates
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

From https://github.com/BtbN/FFmpeg-Builds/issues/505:
> FFmpeg simply did not check the validity of any certificates until yesterday.
  Builds starting from today should finally use the system cert store, though verifying certificates is still turned off by default, since just enabling it would be a breaking change.

Verification can be enabled by passing [`-verify 1`](https://ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg-protocols.html#tls) on the command line.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [ ] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [ ] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

### Complete Verbose Output

```shell

```

author: si70so15

title: Allow metadata to be pruned/filtered
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm requesting a feature unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar requests **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

In [Modifying Metadata](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp?tab=readme-ov-file#modifying-metadata) we have `--parse-metadata` and `--replace-in-metadata` neither allow us to prune particular columns for keeping only specific fields (i.e. no generic catch-all prune) so if for example we want to embed the genre but nothing else we would need to do something like this


```Bash
yt-dlp \
-f "bestaudio" \
--extract-audio \
--audio-format "opus" \
--embed-thumbnail \
--embed-metadata \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_title>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_artist>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_album>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_album_artist>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_date>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_description>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_comment>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_track>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_track_number>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_disc>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_genres>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_composer>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_language>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_show>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_season_number>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_episode_id>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_episode_sort>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_purl>)" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_synopsis>)" \
https://soundcloud.com/soulsequoia/corpse-party
```

Or something like this (although I couldn't get this to work properly):

```bash
yt-dlp \
-f "bestaudio" \
--extract-audio \
--audio-format "opus" \
--embed-thumbnail \
--embed-metadata \
--replace-in-metadata "date,description,synopsis,purl,comments,track,disc,show,season_number,episode_id,episode_sort,comment" ".*" "" \
--parse-metadata ":(?P<meta_comment>)" \
https://soundcloud.com/soulsequoia/corpse-party
```

The above code block would work fine but still some fields are left in example `purl` is specified to be removed but still remains

**CLI output of the code block above's MetadataParser**
```Bash
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a date
[MetadataParser] Changed description to:
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a synopsis
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a purl
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a comments
[MetadataParser] Changed track to:
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a disc
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a show
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a season_number
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a episode_id
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a episode_sort
[MetadataParser] Video does not have a comment
[MetadataParser] Parsed meta_comment from '': ''
```

Yet inside the file we find:
```Bash
Performer                                : soulsequoia， greyy
Genre                                    : Indie Rock
Description                              :  /  /  / 
Recorded date                            : 20250524
Writing application                      : Lavc61.19.101 libopus
Cover                                    : Yes
handler_name                             : SoundHandler
vendor_id                                : [0][0][0][0]
major_brand                              : isom
minor_version                            : 512
compatible_brands                        : isomiso2mp41
synopsis                                 :  /  /  / 
purl                                     : https://soundcloud.com/soulsequoia/corpse-party
```

They are clearly still there, so the feature request is this:

Make a new `metadata option` that is exclusionary by default and will only allow specified fields to be filled from scraped metadata

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [x] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
- [x] Copy the WHOLE output (starting with `[debug] Command-line config`) and insert it below

title: Fails to download tracks from Youtube Music that are not available as a Youtube Video
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

A "video" (actually it is an audio track) is available on Youtube Music but not on regular Youtube. In fact I get a "Video Unavailable" error if I try to access it via the www.youtube.com domain, however, the track works flawlessly in my browser if I access it via the music.youtube.com domain. 
Unfortunately, with `yt-dlp` I am getting the "Video Unavailable" error, which hints that it might be attempting to download it from the www.youtube.com domain, or it is expecting to find/download a "video"

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
