### Region

_No response_

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

yt-dlp tries to download from an inaccessible url (`ERROR: unable to download video data: HTTP Error 403: Forbidden`) when I use this combination of extractor-args: `youtube:player_client=all;formats=missing_pot`
**_Important:_** When using either one individually (`player_client=all` or `formats=missing_pot`), it downloads from good urls.
I have been using this combination since last year without problems, it just started on today's version.

title: [Youtube] 403 forbidden error when using mweb client even with po token
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

United States

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I'm downloading playlists using the mweb client + bgutil po-token generator and am now getting 403 forbidden errors after the signature extraction bug was fixed.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead

title: ATTN: nicolaasjan, your recent Windows XP builds don't work
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

I'm sorry for posting here, but I didn't know how else to bring this to nicolaasjan's attention.

First off, thank you very much for continuing to provide yt-dlp builds for older versions of Windows. I know my system is very outdated, but right now, it's all I have.

That said, none of the recent Windows XP binaries, going back to 8/23 of this year, work. 2025.08.20.060440 is the last release that works on my WinXP system.

2025.08.23.181722 gives the error;

"The procedure entry point QueryWorkingSetEx could not be located in the dynamic link library psapi.dll"

and references python310.dll on the command line.

2025.09.23.052315 gives the same error, but references python311.dll on the command line.

For the older releases, I downloaded the WinXP Zip file. For the newer ones, I downloaded the yt-dlp_x86_winXP.exe file.

title: Cannot write ID3v2 Comment for MP3
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Trying to write *only* ID3v2 tags in an MP3 file. The default behavior with  `-f ba -t mp3` gives an MP3 file with a truncated ID3v1 "comment" as `https://www.youtube.com/watch?`. This is not useful.

This can be accomplished with:
```
yt-dlp -vU --ignore-config -f ba -t mp3 --audio-quality 4 --embed-thumbnail --convert-thumbnails jpg --embed-metadata  'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uK8WgcIP7j4'
```

And verified with:
```
exiftool -G1 ./Airplane\ Take\ Off\ ｜\ Royalty\ Free\ ｜\ CC0\ license\ \[uK8WgcIP7j4\].mp3 | egrep -i comment    
[ID3v2_3]       User Defined Text               : (comment) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uK8WgcIP7j4
[ID3v1]         Comment                         : https://www.youtube.com/watch?
```

Trying to *not* include ID3v1 tags by adding`--ppa "FFmpeg: -write_id3v1 0"`, so ffmpeg defaults to ID3v2 tags, but that's not working.

Command:
```
yt-dlp -vU --ignore-config -f ba -t mp3 --audio-quality 4 --embed-thumbnail --convert-thumbnails jpg --embed-metadata --ppa "FFmpeg: -write_id3v1 0" 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uK8WgcIP7j4'
```

It loses the ID3v1 comment, but fails to write a proper ID3v2 comment:
```
exiftool -G1 ./Best\ Prabhupada\ Kirtan\ Hare\ Krishna\ \[4wQgOLDXeRw\].mp3 | egrep -i comment 
[ID3v2_3]       User Defined Text               : (comment) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4wQgOLDXeRw
```

The `--ppa "FFmpeg: -write_id3v1 0"` should disable ID3v1 tags, and let ffmpeg default to ID3v2 tags.

But, it seems that during the `Metadata` and `EmbedThumbnail` passes, ffmpeg is executed with `-write_id3v1 1`, then it does stuff, then it sets `-write_id3v1 0`.

End result: The "Comment" is missing.

This can be verified with:
(nightly)
```
exiftool -G1 Airplane\ Take\ Off\ ｜\ Royalty\ Free\ ｜\ CC0\ license\ \[uK8WgcIP7j4\].mp3 | egrep -i comment
[ID3v2_3]       User Defined Text               : (comment) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uK8WgcIP7j4
```

This can fix it, and demonstrate how it should be working:
```
eyeD3 --comment=$( mediainfo ./Airplane\ Take\ Off\ ｜\ Royalty\ Free\ ｜\ CC0\ license\ \[uK8WgcIP7j4\].mp3 | egrep '^comment' | awk '{print $3}') Airplane\ Take\ Off\ ｜\ Royalty\ Free\ ｜\ CC0\ license\ \[uK8WgcIP7j4\].mp3
```

```
exiftool -G1 Airplane\ Take\ Off\ ｜\ Royalty\ Free\ ｜\ CC0\ license\ \[uK8WgcIP7j4\].mp3 | egrep -i comment                                                                                                                    
[ID3v2_3]       Comment                         : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uK8WgcIP7j4
[ID3v2_3]       User Defined Text               : (comment) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uK8WgcIP7j4
```

This can also be verified via an MP3 player that can display "comment" metadata.

title: Is yt-dlp playback broken for all clients (android, ios, tv, remix, etc)?
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

Hi,

I'm trying to use yt-dlp with the --extractor-args "youtube:player-client=android" (and many others), but none of them return playable audio or video formats anymore.

What I’ve tested:

--extractor-args "youtube:player-client=android"

ios, tvhtml5, web_remix, web_embedded, web_music, web, etc.

With and without --cookies, --cookies-from-browser, even fresh from Firefox

Valid accounts (no 2FA, no restricted content, no age restrictions)

Results:

Only storyboard formats (sb0/sb1/sb2) are returned

If cookies are used, yt-dlp says:
Skipping client "android" since it does not support cookies

If cookies are not used, we get:
Sign in to confirm you’re not a bot

Also, using yt.player() via Innertube for any of these clients returns responses without streamingData or playbackTracking, just errors like:

{
  "error": {
    "code": 400,
    "message": "Precondition check failed.",
    "status": "FAILED_PRECONDITION"
  }
}


Is there any client+cookie combination that works in 2025?

If this is a known issue, is there a workaround or plan to support it again?

Thanks in advance.

author: dr-Zub99

title: Getting 'No suitable extractor (Soundcloud) found for URL {url}' when using scsearch.
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [ ] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

Italy

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Hi.

I'm working on a Discord music bot that leverages yt-dlp to provide its functionality. One of which is being able to play a track from SoundCloud just by searching for it.

Here's the core of how it works:

```
...
    search_string = query_type[0] # This will either be a URL regex or a yt-dlp search string (scsearch: or ytsearch:)
    try:
        with YoutubeDL(YDL_OPTIONS) as ydl:
            if isinstance(search_string, str):
                info = ydl.extract_info(search_string + query, download=False)
            else:
                info = ydl.extract_info(query, download=False)
    except Exception as e:
        log_to_discord_log(e)

        return Error(f"An internal error occured while extracting `{query[:50]}`. Please try another source website.")
     ...
```

The bot replies with the error above defined. _Only_ SoundCloud search is affected by this. YouTube search or direct URL aren't.

I'm pretty sure my code is not the issue. I've done print debugging and it correctly showed 'scsearch:{query}' (which is how it's supposed to be done according to the docs).

Looking at the logs shows this:

```
yt_dlp.utils.DownloadError: ERROR: No suitable extractor (Soundcloud) found for URL https://api.soundcloud.com/tracks/soundcloud%3Atracks%3A658715087
```

The given URL seems to point to a 401 Unauthorized page. I've updated to the latest version available.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

title: Kick VOD download giving HTTP 403 error
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I'm reporting that yt-dlp is broken on a **supported** site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've checked that all provided URLs are playable in a browser with the same IP and same login details
- [x] I've checked that all URLs and arguments with special characters are [properly quoted or escaped](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ#video-url-contains-an-ampersand--and-im-getting-some-strange-output-1-2839-or-v-is-not-recognized-as-an-internal-or-external-command)
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates
- [x] I've read the [policy against AI/LLM contributions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#automated-contributions-ai--llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [x] I've read about [sharing account credentials](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#are-you-willing-to-share-account-details-if-needed) and I'm willing to share it if required

### Region

global

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

yt-dlp can't download Kick VODs, as accessing the HLS playlist gives a HTTP 403 Forbidden error. Example VOD link: https://kick.com/otakucyclist/videos/ab812165-70f6-4574-855b-2253d0c98340

Trying to download the m3u8 file directly gives a 403 error as well.

I'm located in Hungary.

### Provide verbose output that clearly demonstrates the problem

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)
- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
